content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 WiFi Tribe: Leader for Premium Remote Work + Travel Community (global travel required) Anywhere in the World Delaware Leadership and Relationship Building All Other Remote Contract <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0018/5909/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United Kingdom <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://wifitribe.co">https://wifitribe.co</a> </p> <h3>Become a WiFi Tribe Chapter Host</h3> <p>WiFi Tribe is the vetted community for remote professionals who travel together while they work. For the last 10 years, we have been traveling the world together with our members, living in a different city every month. On weekdays, we work remotely side-by-side, and on the weekends, we go on trips and adventures together.<br><br>Right after the pandemic, the interest in what we do has exploded. We're now expanding our team and looking for talented and passionate community builders to take on the world with us as Chapter Hosts.<br><br>A WiFi Tribe Chapter Host is our version of a community leader. As a Host, you will be traveling the world with 15-25 other WiFi Tribe members and be in charge of the vibe (the culture) and group dynamics. This is a paid job and all travel costs are covered.</p> <h3>Why Does WiFi Tribe Exist?</h3> <p>We all need to feel that we belong somewhere. Humans evolved and survived as tribes and communities. But now, with billions of people living in mega cities and lonely one-person apartments, we're more disconnected than we ever were. We've traded comfort, success, and belongings for what really matters; human connection. Oh, and, where has our sense of adventure and discovery gone?!<br><br>You're reading this because you're a person who derives meaning and energy from being around other great people and bringing them together. You're a people person! You know this, because you've been told so often by others that you're just... <em>naturally good with people</em>. And, I'm going to bet that you love a good adventure.<br><br>Maybe, you're in a moment of transition in your life, where you're asking yourself: <em>"What matters to me? What do I want more of in my life? What do I want my life to look like? And what do I need to change to get it there?"</em><br><br>At WiFi Tribe, we believe that a rich, fulfilling life boils down to three things:</p> <ol> <li> <p>a collection of shared experiences</p> </li> <li> <p>deep, meaningful relationships, and</p> </li> <li> <p>the sense of purpose we find when we are making a real difference in other people's lives.</p> </li> </ol> <h3>What Does a Chapter Host Do?</h3> <p>Our Chapter Hosts are the most important members of our team. Everyone else on the team is here to support <em>you</em> in making the magic happen! As a Host, your mission is to strengthen the culture and foster a tight-knit community. The result of that will be a mind-expanding experience that bonds people together for life.<br><br><em>Challenge accepted?<br></em><br>Ok, here's how it will happen... You'll be traveling the world with 20 other freelancers, entrepreneurs, and remote professionals, living and working together from a new city every month. Yes, you'll be responsible for the logistics of setting up a new destination, <em>but that's the easy part</em>... You'll find the ultimate match to your people skills and your natural leadership in the moments where you need to bring this group of 20 together and foster a real sense of community, wherever in the world we go.<br><br>As a Host, you are both the community architect and the community builder. You are a leader who empowers others to become active contributors and leaders too – an engineer of human dynamics. Most importantly, <em>you are a champion for the culture</em> WiFi Tribe has carefully fostered over the last five years.<br><br>And when necessary, you step up to ensure that the experience is safe for everyone. You're the person in charge if anything needs to get resolved, and the on-the-ground liaison with the landlord/property owner. And don't worry, the rest of the WiFi Tribe team has got your back – we're here to help you make the magic happen every step of the way!</p> <h3>How, Exactly, Will I Be Working?</h3> <p>Hosts usually run two or three Chapters in a row. They are then off for one or two months before hosting their next set of Chapters. Each Chapter is four or six weeks long, so a Host is typically running Chapters for two or three months at a time.<br><br>There is always a week in-between each Chapter. We call it the ‘gap week’. Hosts arrive at their destinations at the start of the gap week so that they have enough time to prepare for their next Chapter.<br><br>The Chapter Host role is a 12-month contract that is paid per completed Chapter. Whenever Hosts are not running a Chapter, there are no work-related expectations from our side. Therefore, we recommend applicants to have other part-time work alongside the Host role. Ideally, you would either already be working as a freelancer, have a part-time remote job, or be running your own company to have a stable income, especially during the months when you’re not running a Chapter.<br><br>When on-Chapter, you can expect a part-time commitment (about 20h per week, with most of those hours at the beginning of the Chapter and less at the end) and when off-Chapter, you wouldn’t have any WiFi Tribe-related work.<br><br>There may be an opportunity to take on additional projects within the WiFi Tribe company at a later time. This would depend on your skills, work experience, and interests in other areas of our company, as well as what kind of projects are available. Please do not rely on this, as we cannot guarantee that there will be sufficient projects to support your full-time income needs.</p> <h3>Am I the Right Person for this Role?</h3> <p>Our application process takes a lot of time and effort and we've had 200 applicants per available spot. So, out of respect to your time, we want to help you self-assess whether you are exactly the kind of person we're looking for:</p> <ol> <li> <p><strong>You're a natural leader.</strong> Somehow people always gravitate around you and look to you for decisions.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>You're confident.</strong> You're very comfortable being authentic self and your are stable within yourself.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>You're brave.</strong> You don't shy away from doing the right thing, even when it's hard to do.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>You're Reliable:</strong> People quickly trust you because you move through life with integrity.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>You can 'feel' the room.</strong> You're aware of how everyone is feeling and you always make sure that everyone is involved and having a good time.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>You're a people person.</strong> Your friends and colleagues always tell you that you're really good with people!</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>You're a community builder.</strong> You naturally bring groups of
